traceRefinementTailStatus.label: trace derivative geometric refinement tail
traceRefinementTailStatus.reason: Closed when the 80:80->90:90 trace-derivative difference, plus a geometric tail inferred from the previous refinement step, is below the chosen trace-radius target at every collocation node.

rTailStatus.label: r>=12 confluent integral tail
rTailStatus.reason: Closed when absolute panel integrals beyond r=12, plus a geometric beyond-last-panel tail, are below the final coefficient interval radius scale.

eigenrowStabilityStatus.label: confluent eigenrow stability
eigenrowStabilityStatus.reason: Closed numerically when the negative eigenvalue remains simple with a visible gap and sign alignment is stable across all collocation nodes and refinement levels.

coefficientPropagationStatus.label: exact finite Krawczyk coefficient input
coefficientPropagationStatus.reason: Closed only after the finite Krawczyk budget comparison and the refinement tail model are both closed.  The remaining external proof obligation is to justify the refinement tail from analytic quadrature/eigenrow perturbation estimates.

fullRankShortcutStatus.label: endpoint full-rank shortcut from coefficient enclosure
fullRankShortcutStatus.reason: If the coefficient intervals are exact, the Krawczyk theorem keeps the endpoint map in the persistent p_01 chart, so the active endpoint map has full row rank and the Green BVP has no active compatibility obstruction.

finiteTraceTailCertificateStatus.label: finite trace-tail certificate feeding Krawczyk
finiteTraceTailCertificateStatus.reason: Closed when trace refinement, r-tail, eigenrow stability, and final coefficient propagation are all closed.

strictMachineRigorousStatus.label: strict interval/Bernstein machine proof
strictMachineRigorousStatus.reason: Still open.  This script supplies the finite refinement-tail certificate and the r-tail panel bound.  A strict machine proof would replace the geometric quadrature-refinement model by interval or Bernstein derivative bounds for each Gauss-Legendre segment.

conclusion: The confluent trace data, r-tail, eigenrow stability, and final Krawczyk propagation are consistent with the endpoint full-rank shortcut by a huge margin.  The remaining formal hardening is the pure quadrature-error theorem for the explicit confluent integrand.
